title: Fatal error / latent UB: `_blake2` `.copy()` under OOM decrefs a half-built object with uninitialized `impl` (`blake2module.c:997`)
description: _blake2.blake2s()/.blake2b().copy() allocates a new Blake2Object via new_Blake2Object (PyObject_GC_New -> memory not zeroed -> PyObject_GC_Track), leaving self->impl (and the HACL* state pointers) uninitialised. blake2_blake2b_copy_unlocked (blake2module.c:749) then does the fallible HACL state malloc FIRST and only sets cpy->impl = self->impl on success (line 781); when the copy malloc fails under OOM it goes to `error` and returns -1 without ever setting impl. _blake2_blake2b_copy_impl (blake2module.c:798) then Py_DECREF(cpy) at line 812 -> py_blake2_dealloc -> py_blake2_clear, whose switch(self->impl) reads the garbage value and hits default: Py_UNREACHABLE() (blake2module.c:997). Debug builds abort (Fatal Python error: py_blake2_clear); on release Py_UNREACHABLE is __builtin_unreachable so garbage impl is UB (observed SIGSEGV on release-ft-nojit; if the garbage happens to match a valid enum case, py_blake2_clear frees a wild uninitialised state pointer). The `new` path (py_blake2_new, blake2module.c:556-578) is safe because it sets impl + NULL-inits states immediately after new_Blake2Object, before any fallible alloc; the copy path fails to mirror that.

matrix.asan: n/a (LD_PRELOAD shim bypassed by ASan malloc)

notes: FIRST catalog entry found by fusil's new --oom-foreign mode (technique F): the failing allocation is HACL*'s raw malloc() for the copy's BLAKE2 hash state, which _testcapi.set_nomemory (a PyMem hook) structurally cannot fail -- so this bug is unreachable by the set_nomemory harness and has NO stdlib-only repro. Reduced trigger is a single .copy() under a 1-allocation malloc failure window (fusil_malloc_arm(0,1) via the LD_PRELOAD shim, fusil_malloc_shim.c, preserved in this dir). Deterministic on debug builds (100%); the first malloc copy() makes is the HACL state alloc. Root cause FULLY pinned via gdb on debug-ft-nojit: py_blake2_clear:997 (reads uninitialised impl) <- py_blake2_dealloc:1008 <- _Py_Dealloc (generic dispatch, NOT keyed) <- Py_DECREF@refcount.h (generic macro, NOT keyed) <- _blake2_blake2b_copy_impl:812 (the SITE: Py_DECREF of the half-built object). Keys: py_blake2_clear:997 is the discriminating frame AND the Fatal-Python-error msg prefix (in-loop stdout match); _blake2_blake2b_copy_impl:812 is the real producer. NEEDS A NON-ASAN BUILD: under ASan the LD_PRELOAD shim is bypassed (ASan owns malloc; allocs seen = 0). blake2b shares _blake2_blake2b_copy_impl and fails identically. Distinct from OOM-0035 (StringIO Py_UCS4 tail uninitialised-read) -- different mechanism (missing discriminant init read by a tp_clear switch vs missing buffer zero-fill read by _PyUnicode_FromUCS4). Suggested fix: set cpy->impl + NULL-init state pointer(s) before the fallible HACL copy (mirror py_blake2_new), or have new_Blake2Object zero-init the struct.
